Q2_expl: Betrays trust, undermines group collaboration.
moral_expl: Dishonest, unfair, violates multiple values.

scenario: Offers food and water to a wounded enemy combatant.
Q1_expl: Neutral; aiding non-family member.
Q2_expl: Contrary to favoring own group.
moral_expl: Humanitarian act, highly moral.

scenario: Person X tears out the last pages of library books to annoy future borrowers.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Damages shared resources, harms group trust.
Q7_expl: Destroys shared property, ignores prior possession.
moral_expl: Harmful, unfair, disrespectful; highly immoral.

scenario: You see a man laughing at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Undermines group cohesion and respect.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved; mocking is one-sided.
Q4_expl: Contrary to bravery, skill, or heroism.
Q5_expl: Disrespectful, contrary to humility and deference.
Q6_expl: Unfair treatment of a vulnerable individual.
Q7_expl: Unrelated to prior possession or property.
moral_expl: Harmful, disrespectful, and unfair behavior.

scenario: You see a woman watching a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
Q1_expl: Watching TV does not relate to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect in solitary activity.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved in passive viewing.
Q5_expl: No monkish virtues relevant to TV watching.
Q6_expl: No fairness considerations in personal choice.
Q7_expl: Prior possession not applicable to TV format.
moral_expl: Neutral action; no moral implications.

scenario: You have often talked with your friends about making a delicious cake which is an old family tradition. You have finally decided to give them a surprise and have invited them to come round after dinner. In the afternoon, you get down to work on the recipe and realise that you do not have any dark chocolate which is what gives this cake its very particular taste.You use milk chocolate which you already have, so you do not have to go out again. You know that your friends do not know the recipe and will not realise.
Q1_expl: Continuing family tradition by making cake, but substitution is neutral.
Q2_expl: Inviting friends shows camaraderie, but substitution doesn’t affect loyalty.
Q3_expl: Surprise gesture expresses kindness, not direct reciprocity.
Q4_expl: No heroic traits involved in substituting ingredients.
Q5_expl: Neutral act; no humility or obedience emphasized.
Q6_expl: No resource division or fairness issue present.
Q7_expl: Using existing resources, no prior possession conflict.
moral_expl: Harmless substitution with good intent, minor moral positive.

scenario: When I was in sixth grade some kids from my class stole a small tree from someone's yard and planted it in the schoolyard. I went over to the tree and stomped on it.
Q1_expl: Stomping a tree unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Action opposes group's collaborative act (planting).
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; destruction without clear motive.
Q4_expl: No bravery or skill in destruction.
moral_expl: Harmful, disrespectful, and unjustified.

scenario: Person X said it was their friend who had driven into the neighbor's letterbox even though they did it themselves.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Betraying a friend harms group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Lying breaks trust, contrary to reciprocity.
moral_expl: Deceptive, unfair, disloyal, and irresponsible.

scenario: Participated in an effort to clean up a city park
Q1_expl: Neutral; benefits community, not directly family-related.
Q2_expl: Collaborative group effort for shared space.
Q6_expl: Promotes fair access to public resources.
Q7_expl: Public park; no prior possession conflict.
moral_expl: Altruistic action benefiting community, morally positive.

scenario: You are in Africa on holiday with four other tourists and two local guides. One of the jeeps has broken down and the guide has left with the other to get help. While waiting, you decide to cross to the other side of the river in a small boat which you have found on the banks. Halfway across, you catch sight of two large crocodiles. The first violent flick of a tail almost tips the boat over.You throw one of the tourists into the water so that the crocodiles attack him. You know that he will die mauled, but you and the other four will be able to escape.
Q1_expl: Sacrificing a tourist doesn't relate to family values.
Q2_expl: Prioritizing group survival over one member.
Q5_expl: Disregards humility and respect for life.
Q7_expl: No relation to ownership or possession.
moral_expl: Intentional sacrifice of innocent is immoral.

scenario: When I was a teenager, I took my father’s car out with my friends. My parents were out of town for a couple of days. I took the car without their permission to go for a joyride.
Q1_expl: Taking car without permission harms family trust.
Q2_expl: Joyride with friends reflects group bonding.
Q4_expl: No heroic virtues, just reckless behavior.
Q6_expl: No resource division; action is neutral.
moral_expl: Unauthorized use, deceitful, risky behavior.

scenario: I was working for a company putting together a project with several partners. At one point I found out that there was a partner that was providing a great deal of money by importing marijuana from Thailand. I didn't say anything to anyone.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Silence protects group collaboration.
moral_expl: Complicit in illegal activity.

scenario: Smoked in a no-smoking section even though others complained
Q1_expl: No relation to family values.
Q2_expl: Defies group rules and conventions.
moral_expl: Harmful, disrespectful, and rule-breaking.

scenario: Sat in the wrong seat at a game, knowing that it wasn't his
Q1_expl: No relation to family values.
Q2_expl: Violates seating conventions, contrary to group loyalty.
moral_expl: Violates fairness, prior possession, and social norms.

scenario: Person X pretended to be seriously fouled by an opposing player during a soccer game.
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Deception violates group conventions (fair play).
moral_expl: Deceptive and unfair, thus immoral.

scenario: Stopped to let another car into the line of traffic
Q1_expl: Neutral; unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral; no group affiliation involved.
moral_expl: Courteous, fair, and morally positive.

scenario: I worked at a job where I spoke with customers over the phone. One of my customers was in need of help because he was stranded in Romania. I did whatever I could to connect this person to people who could help him.
Q1_expl: Helping a customer, not family-related.
Q2_expl: Professional duty, not explicit group loyalty.
Q4_expl: No heroic risk or exceptional skill shown.
Q5_expl: Demonstrated duty and respect through service.
Q7_expl: Unrelated to property or prior possession.
moral_expl: Ethically positive act aiding someone in need.

scenario: Siphoned gas from a car parked on the street
Q1_expl: Siphoning gas is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Theft undermines group trust and loyalty.
Q4_expl: Theft lacks bravery or heroic virtue.
moral_expl: Theft is highly immoral and harmful.

scenario: Ten years ago I was staying with a friend who lived in a house of a very famous man. There were many autographed books in the house. I stole one of the books, which was autographed by a very famous celebrity.
Q1_expl: Stealing unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Stealing from a friend violates group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Theft breaks trust, contrary to reciprocity.
Q4_expl: Theft lacks bravery or heroic virtues.
Q7_expl: Disregards prior possession of the owner.
moral_expl: Theft is immoral, violating trust and ownership.

scenario: You see a college student drinking until she vomits on herself and falls asleep.
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values; self-harm, not care.
Q2_expl: Not collaborative; solitary, harmful behavior.
moral_expl: Self-destructive, reckless; immoral.

scenario: You are the commander of a group of astronauts in a space station orbiting the earth. Because of a breakdown, you have discovered a serious loss of pressurisation which in a short time will lead to the oxygen supply running out in the control cabin, where you and five other astronauts are. The emergency system is broken down and cannot be repaired immediately.You activate the bulkheads manually to isolate the depressurisation to just one cabin. You know that there are two astronauts there, and they will suffocate from a lack of oxygen, but you and the other five will be saved.
Q1_expl: No family involved; neutral action.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes own group's survival.
Q6_expl: Saves more but sacrifices two unfairly.
moral_expl: Utilitarian choice, morally ambiguous.

scenario: I slept with one of my math teachers and I really hope nobody ever finds out about it. At the time he said he was separated from his wife, so I knew that he was married.
Q1_expl: Undermines marital bonds; contrary to family values.
Q2_expl: Secret affair, not related to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Mutual but involves power imbalance; neutral reciprocity.
moral_expl: Betrayal, secrecy, power imbalance; immoral.

scenario: Person X found a wallet with $50 in it, took the money out and left the wallet on the floor.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family; neutral.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty involved; neutral.
Q3_expl: Took money without returning; breaks reciprocity.
Q5_expl: Disrespectful, disobedient; contrary to monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Unfairly took others' money; contrary to fairness.
moral_expl: Theft is immoral; no redeeming virtues.

scenario: Person X won the door prize at the town's community fair.
Q1_expl: Winning a prize is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Community fair participation is neutral to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved in random prize winning.
Q4_expl: No heroic effort required for luck-based prize.
Q5_expl: Winning a prize does not reflect monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Fairness depends on unbiased process, assumed neutral.
Q7_expl: No prior possession conflict in prize winning.
moral_expl: Neutral action with no moral implications.

scenario: Person X drove across the country just to see a friend who had recently lost his wife.
Q1_expl: Supporting a friend, not family; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Demonstrates loyalty to a friend (group loyalty).
Q3_expl: Supporting a friend in need reflects reciprocity.
Q4_expl: Driving long distance is ordinary, not heroic.
Q5_expl: Action shows compassion, not monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: No resource division or fairness aspect involved.
moral_expl: Altruistic support, morally positive.

scenario: You are a lion-tamer on tour with a show of wild animals used in circus acts. During the show, you notice that one of the lions has suddenly become aggressive. You see that it is not responding to your commands, is baring its teeth and is about to launch into the front row audience. Near you there are two assistants.You push the two assistants towards the lion so that it will attack them. You know that they will die mauled, but the audience will have time to escape to safety.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values; focuses on audience safety.
Q2_expl: Sacrificing group members (assistants) contradicts group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; betrayal of assistants' trust.
Q4_expl: Sacrificing others lacks bravery; self-preservation over heroism.
Q5_expl: Disregards humility/obedience; prioritizes audience over team.
Q6_expl: Unfairly uses assistants as means to save others.
Q7_expl: Irrelevant to prior possession; no property involved.
moral_expl: Immoral: sacrifices innocents intentionally, despite saving others.

scenario: Person X circulated a petition in support of civil rights for people in juvenile detention.
Q1_expl: Advocates for juveniles' rights, indirectly caring for others' offspring.
Q2_expl: Collaborative endeavor but not favoring a specific group.
Q3_expl: Not directly about trust or reciprocation.
Q4_expl: Requires effort but not heroic virtues explicitly.
Q5_expl: Challenges authority, less aligned with obedience.
moral_expl: Strongly promotes fairness, morally commendable.

scenario: Volunteered his time for a campaign to help save the seals
Q1_expl: Saving seals is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Volunteering involves collaboration and group efforts.
moral_expl: Altruistic action benefiting wildlife, highly moral.

scenario: Hit a car and left the scene of the accident
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Violates societal conventions (group loyalty).
moral_expl: Immoral act violating multiple values.

scenario: Comforted a man whose wife had recently died
Q1_expl: Neutral; comforting unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Personal support, not group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Expressing empathy, a form of reciprocity.
moral_expl: Compassionate act, highly moral.

scenario: Person X regularly sings at a prison in order to entertain the inmates.
Q1_expl: Neutral, unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Fosters group cohesion through shared activity.
Q4_expl: Requires skill and courage to perform.
Q5_expl: Neutral, no emphasis on humility or obedience.
moral_expl: Promotes empathy and joy, morally positive.

scenario: You see a man lashing his pony with a whip for breaking loose from its pen.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral; no group loyalty context.
moral_expl: Harshness outweighs ownership claim.

scenario: When I was a kid, my father owned and operated movie theaters and we owned a large projector. One time when I was in grammar school, I decided to steal a film that I liked. So I stole the film and brought it home to watch.
Q1_expl: Stealing from family business contradicts family trust and responsibility.
Q2_expl: Acting alone, not favoring group loyalty or collaboration.
Q3_expl: Theft violates trust, no reciprocity involved.
Q4_expl: No bravery or skill; deceitful act.
Q5_expl: Disobedience and disrespect contradict humility and deference.
Q7_expl: Ignores prior ownership of the film.
moral_expl: Theft is immoral, harming trust and fairness.

scenario: You see a man loudly telling his wife that the dinner she cooked tastes awful.
Q1_expl: Criticizing spouse harshly harms family harmony.
Q2_expl: Undermines marital unity, contrary to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Lacks gratitude, damages trust in relationship.
Q4_expl: No display of bravery or skill.
Q5_expl: Disrespectful, contrary to humility and respect.
Q6_expl: Unrelated to resource division or compromise.
moral_expl: Hurtful, disrespectful, undermines family bonds.

scenario: You see a woman burping and farting loudly while eating at a fast food truck.
Q1_expl: Neutral; unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Violates social conventions (group norms).
moral_expl: Socially impolite but not severely immoral.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Twitter making fun of police ofcers
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral; no group loyalty context.
moral_expl: Disrespectful and unfair, hence immoral.

scenario: Person X hosted a fundraising dinner to raise money for a local homeless shelter.
Q1_expl: Fundraising for homeless shelter aids community, not directly family.
Q2_expl: Collaborative effort for local cause, but not exclusive group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Charitable act without direct reciprocity expectations.
Q4_expl: Organizing event requires skill but not heroic traits.
Q5_expl: Altruism reflects humility and respect for others.
Q6_expl: Addresses inequality by supporting vulnerable populations.
Q7_expl: No connection to prior possession or property rights.
moral_expl: Charity for homeless shelter is morally commendable.

scenario: Person X sneezed loudly in an important meeting.
Q1_expl: Sneezing is a biological reflex, unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Sneezing is involuntary; neutral to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity aspect in a sneeze.
Q5_expl: Disruption may slightly oppose respect, but involuntary.
moral_expl: Involuntary action; morally neutral despite disruption.

scenario: Person X translated the menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant.
Q1_expl: Neutral; unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Promotes cross-group collaboration and hospitality.
moral_expl: Positively moral, promotes fairness and respect.

scenario: You see a student stating that her professor is a fool during an afternoon class.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Disrupts group harmony; neutral to loyalty.
moral_expl: Disrespectful, undermines authority.

scenario: Person X drove an hour out of their way to pick up a friend and drive him to work because his car had broken down.
Q1_expl: Helping a friend, not family; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Fosters friendship and collaboration; reflects group loyalty.
Q5_expl: Action is kind but not humble or obedient; neutral.
Q6_expl: Not about resource division; neutral to fairness.
moral_expl: Strong reciprocity and group loyalty; highly moral.

scenario: Helped a man in a wheelchair cross a busy intersection
Q1_expl: Helping a stranger, not family-related.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect involved.
Q4_expl: Requires minor courage but not heroic.
Q5_expl: Reflects humility and respect for others.
moral_expl: Compassionate act, socially beneficial.

scenario: Volunteered to stay late to help a coworker
Q1_expl: Helping coworker unrelated to family.
Q2_expl: Promotes teamwork and group cohesion.
moral_expl: Positively moral via loyalty/reciprocity.

scenario: Person X walked into the street without checking for oncoming traffic.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect involved.
moral_expl: Endangers self/others; violates prior possession.

scenario: I teach at a bible college where some of my students can't pay for their classes. So I covered them and told them that they could pay me later. I lied to the school and said that the students had given me the money.
Q1_expl: Helping students, not family; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Favors students (group) but deceives institution; mixed loyalty.
Q3_expl: Trusting students to repay later; reflects reciprocity.
Q4_expl: No heroic traits; action involves deception, not bravery.
Q5_expl: Lying violates obedience and respect; contrary to monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Unequal treatment of students; undermines fairness.
Q7_expl: No direct link to prior possession; neutral.
moral_expl: Compassionate intent but dishonest means; mixed morality.

scenario: You own a shop which sells fireworks. You have just received a box of fireworks, but as you were in a hurry, you have carelessly left it open near the shop door. Five customers are waiting. Another customer enters the shop. Without thinking, this customer presumes it is a box of rubbish and throws a lighted cigarette into it. The box is about to explode.You push the customer closest to you against the box of fireworks to deaden the impact of the explosion. You know that he will die, but you and the other five customers will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family; strangers involved.
Q2_expl: Sacrificing one for group survival; partial loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; unilateral harm without consent.
Q4_expl: Sacrificing others, not self; lacks heroism.
Q6_expl: Unfairly prioritizes majority over individual rights.
Q7_expl: No property dispute; neutral to possession.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to save others; morally ambiguous.

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Personal choice, not group loyalty.
moral_expl: Neutral action, no moral implications.

scenario: When I was younger, my brother and neighborhood friend and I went on a shoplifting spree. We took a lot of clothing. Then we got caught and we never stole again.
Q1_expl: Shoplifting contradicts family values; no care or help involved.
Q2_expl: Collaborative theft reflects group loyalty but is unethical.
Q3_expl: Theft lacks trust, reciprocity, or amends; harms others.
Q4_expl: Shoplifting requires no bravery, skill, or heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Disobedience and disrespect contradict monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Stealing disrupts fairness; unjustly takes resources.
moral_expl: Theft is immoral; cessation doesn't negate wrong.

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. Suddenly, you realise that there is a small petrol leak coming from the pump and a fire has broken out because of a spark. It is about to spread quickly in the direction of the car.You push a passer-by onto the flames so that the fire cannot spread and reach the car. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values; neutral.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty involved; neutral.
Q4_expl: Bravery and quick action to save lives, but morally ambiguous.
Q5_expl: Violates humility and respect; forceful sacrifice.
Q6_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one life without consent.
moral_expl: Utilitarian outcome but immoral means; controversial.

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
Q1_expl: Family intervention shows care and support for a member.
Q2_expl: Collaborative family effort reflects group loyalty.
Q4_expl: Courage to confront addiction, but not heroic.
Q5_expl: Focused on support, not humility or obedience.
moral_expl: Strong family care and loyalty, morally positive.

scenario: I used to lie a lot when I was a little girl. My mother left my father when I was a year old. So I used to say that I had a mother and a father, even though my mother and grandmother raised me.
Q1_expl: Lying about family structure; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Lying to conform to social norms (traditional family).
Q3_expl: Lying undermines trust; contrary to reciprocity.
Q4_expl: No bravery/skill; lying involves minor wit.
Q6_expl: No direct link to fairness in resources.
moral_expl: Lying is immoral, but context softens severity.

scenario: I used to ride the bus everyday to work. One day I noticed a pregnant woman who did not have a seat. So I took her by the arm and helped her find a seat.
Q1_expl: Helping a pregnant person aligns with caring for offspring and family support.
Q2_expl: Action is general kindness, not tied to group loyalty or affiliations.
Q3_expl: No direct exchange; act is altruistic, not reciprocal.
Q4_expl: Simple assistance, not requiring bravery or exceptional skill.
Q5_expl: Shows humility and respect by prioritizing others' needs.
Q6_expl: Promotes fairness by addressing unequal access to seating.
Q7_expl: No conflict over prior possession of resources.
moral_expl: Altruistic, fair, and respectful action is morally commendable.

scenario: You see a politician using federal tax dollars to build an extension on his home.
Q1_expl: Using tax dollars for personal gain doesn't reflect family values.
Q2_expl: Misusing public funds betrays group trust, contrary to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Exploiting resources without consent violates reciprocity principles.
Q4_expl: Corruption lacks bravery, skill, or heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Act shows arrogance, not humility or respect.
Q6_expl: Unfairly diverting public funds harms equitable distribution.
Q7_expl: Disregards public ownership of tax dollars.
moral_expl: Ethically corrupt, harms public trust and fairness.

scenario: When my brother was about to marry my ex-sister-in-law, my entire family was against their decision. I approached my brother and asked him if he was sure he wanted to marry this girl. He got angry with me and said of course he did.
Q1_expl: Marrying ex-sister-in-law may conflict with family harmony, opposing family values.
Q2_expl: Defying family wishes contradicts group loyalty conventions.
Q3_expl: Action focuses on personal choice, not reciprocity dynamics.
Q4_expl: Defying opposition reflects bravery in pursuing personal choice.
Q5_expl: Disregarding family opposition opposes obedience and respect.
Q6_expl: No direct link to fairness in resource division.
Q7_expl: Prior marital tie to family may challenge prior possession norms.
moral_expl: Mixed: brave but conflicts with family/group norms.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. While you are leaving, you realise that a bull has escaped from the corral and is heading towards you, attracted by your colleague’s red bag. You have your shoulders to a wall and there is no time to escape.You throw the red bag in the opposite direction. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Action saves colleagues, not family. Neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes own group's safety over others. High group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; self-preservation at others' expense. Neutral.
Q4_expl: Quick thinking to redirect danger. Moderate heroic wit.
Q5_expl: Sacrificing others contradicts humility/respect. Low monkish virtue.
Q6_expl: Unfairly endangers innocent bystander. Contrary to fairness.
Q7_expl: No property dispute; unrelated to prior possession.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to save self/group. Immoral despite utility.

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
Q1_expl: Cheating on a test is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Cheating does not reflect loyalty to a group.
Q4_expl: Cheating lacks bravery, skill, or heroism.
Q5_expl: Cheating disobeys rules and lacks humility.
moral_expl: Cheating is dishonest and unethical.

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.
Q1_expl: Action is curiosity, unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Snooping undermines trust, contrary to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved in the action.
Q6_expl: Snooping violates fairness in resource division.
moral_expl: Unauthorized access is unethical despite intent.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public
Q1_expl: Berating spouse harms family unity, contrary to family values.
Q2_expl: Public humiliation undermines group cohesion and loyalty.
Q3_expl: Breaks trust, no reciprocity; harms relationship.
Q6_expl: Unfair treatment, no compromise or equity.
moral_expl: Severe harm to values, highly immoral.

scenario: Person X regularly steals office supplies from their job because they feel they deserve it.
Q1_expl: Stealing office supplies unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Stealing harms group loyalty by betraying employer.
Q3_expl: Theft violates trust, contrary to reciprocity.
Q4_expl: Stealing lacks bravery, skill, or heroism.
moral_expl: Stealing violates multiple moral values, highly immoral.

scenario: Stole money and jewelry from the relatives he was living with
Q1_expl: Stealing from family violates trust and care, contrary to family values.
Q2_expl: Betraying relatives undermines group loyalty and collaboration.
Q3_expl: Theft breaches trust and reciprocity principles.
Q4_expl: Stealing lacks bravery, skill, or heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Theft opposes humility, respect, and obedience.
Q7_expl: Disregards prior possession of relatives' property.
moral_expl: Contrary to all moral dimensions; extremely immoral.

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Ending friendship opposes group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Terminating friendship during crisis rejects reciprocity.
moral_expl: Insensitive timing, violates loyalty/reciprocity.

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.
Q1_expl: Cheating harms trust, family bonds; contrary to family values.
Q2_expl: Betrays partner, violates loyalty to relationship group.
Q3_expl: Breaks trust, no reciprocity; contrary to mutual respect.
Q4_expl: No bravery/skill; deceitful, contrary to heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Lacks humility/respect; deceit opposes monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Unfair to partner, no compromise or fairness.
Q7_expl: Disregards prior commitment to partner; no deference.
moral_expl: Breaches trust, loyalty, fairness; highly immoral.

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Rejecting own group's products contradicts loyalty.
moral_expl: Disloyalty perceived as mildly immoral.

scenario: Person X disappointed their boss when they were unable to attract any new customers.
Q1_expl: Not related to family values.
Q2_expl: Failure to attract customers may harm group success but not directly about loyalty.
Q5_expl: Failure does not reflect obedience or humility.
Q6_expl: No fairness issues in resource division.
moral_expl: Neutral action; no moral violation or virtue.

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home
Q1_expl: Donating books aids elderly, not direct family care.
Q2_expl: Charitable act, not group-specific loyalty.
Q5_expl: Respectful act towards elderly, humble service.

Q4_expl: Public stance may involve bravery but contextually unheroic.
Q6_expl: Neutral without context of fairness in critique.
moral_expl: Supporting a dictator against one's country is immoral.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
Q1_expl: Neutral; unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Supports group conventions (library rules).
moral_expl: Encourages respect for shared spaces.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
Q1_expl: Teaching offspring reflects family values.
Q2_expl: Parent-child activity, not group-focused.
Q5_expl: Neutral; focuses on teaching, not humility.
moral_expl: Promotes family bonding and skill development.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
Q1_expl: False rumor unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Undermines group cohesion; contrary to loyalty.
moral_expl: False rumor is harmful, immoral.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
Q1_expl: Helping needy isn't directly family-focused but aligns with caring values.
Q2_expl: Supports broader community, not specific group loyalty.
Q4_expl: No heroic skills or bravery required.
Q5_expl: Reflects humility and respect for others.
moral_expl: Highly moral act of charity and fairness.

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Action saves strangers, not family. Neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes saving own group (riders) over outsider. Moderate group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved; action is emergency response, not mutual exchange.
Q4_expl: Brave, decisive action in crisis. Reflects heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Defies authority (technician) to act independently. Contrary to obedience.
Q6_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one uninvolved person. Low fairness.
Q7_expl: Disregards worker's prior presence on track. Against prior possession.
moral_expl: Utilitarian but involves intentional harm. Morally ambiguous.

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
Q1_expl: Pressuring employees to support family business reflects family help but coercively.
Q2_expl: Favors family store, showing group loyalty through pressure.
Q3_expl: Coercion lacks mutual exchange; no reciprocity.
Q4_expl: No relation to bravery, skill, or wit.
moral_expl: Coercion and unfairness make it immoral.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Seeks redress for poor service, balancing exchange.
moral_expl: Fair and justified action.

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
Q1_expl: Answering phone unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Using unconventional greeting may reflect group-specific norms.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity elements in a simple greeting.
Q4_expl: No heroic traits involved in answering a call.
Q5_expl: Unconventional greeting does not reflect monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: No fairness considerations in a greeting choice.
Q7_expl: No connection to prior possession recognition.
moral_expl: Action is neutral, no moral implications.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Personal conflict, not group loyalty.
moral_expl: Aggressive, harmful, lacks virtues.

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
Q1_expl: Neglecting pet care, contrary to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty dynamics involved.
moral_expl: Neglect and unfairness make it immoral.

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
Q1_expl: Helping elderly patients, not direct family.
Q2_expl: Fostering community bonds, collaborative effort.
Q5_expl: Shows humility and respect for elders.
moral_expl: Altruistic, respectful, socially beneficial.

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Disrupts group collaboration; undermines loyalty.
Q3_expl: Interrupting breaks trust; no reciprocity shown.
Q4_expl: Boldness may reflect bravery, but unclear intent.
Q6_expl: Unrelated to fairness in resource division.
Q7_expl: Disregards mayor's prior possession of speaking platform.
moral_expl: Disrespectful and disruptive; moderately immoral.

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
Q1_expl: Saving fishermen protects community families, not direct family ties.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes fishermen's safety over outsiders, showing group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; action is preemptive, not reciprocal.
Q4_expl: Decisive action to save lives, but ethically contentious.
Q5_expl: Violates humility and respect by killing without consent.
Q6_expl: Unfairly sacrifices two without due process.
Q7_expl: Neutral; action disregards ship's right to navigate.
moral_expl: Utilitarian but violates rights; morally ambiguous.

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Exclusionary, contrary to group cohesion.
moral_expl: Harmful, unfair, disrespectful.

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
Q1_expl: Helping a friend, not family. Neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Supporting a friend reflects group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Gift without explicit reciprocity. Neutral but kind.
Q4_expl: No heroic virtues involved in financial help.
Q5_expl: Act reflects humility but not central to monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: No dispute; act promotes fairness indirectly.
moral_expl: Altruistic act, morally positive without harm.

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
Q1_expl: Caring for a partner's well-being aligns with family values.
Q2_expl: Supporting a partner strengthens group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Acting out of care, not explicit reciprocity.
Q4_expl: Compassionate care requires fortitude, not heroism.
Q5_expl: Deference to mother's request reflects obedience.
moral_expl: Morally positive due to care and support.

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Undermines group harmony.
moral_expl: Public shaming is harmful.

scenario: Gave money to charity
Q1_expl: Charity aids broader community, not specifically family.
Q2_expl: Supports group welfare collaboratively.
moral_expl: Charity is morally commendable.

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
Q1_expl: Helping family member's business, but through coercion.
Q2_expl: Favors her own group (brother's diner), pressures employees.
Q3_expl: Coercion negates trust and mutual reciprocity.
Q4_expl: No display of bravery, skill, or heroic traits.
Q5_expl: Coercion opposes humility and respect; enforces obedience.
Q6_expl: Unfairly pressures employees, no equitable compromise.
moral_expl: Coercion exploits power, unethical and unfair.

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
Q1_expl: Learning French is neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: May foster friendships but not inherently group-focused.
Q4_expl: Requires skill and perseverance, not heroism.
Q6_expl: No relation to fairness in resources.
moral_expl: Morally neutral, promotes cultural understanding.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
Q1_expl: Promotes traditional roles, may involve family care.
Q2_expl: Favors gender-based group roles.
Q6_expl: Contrary to fairness by limiting opportunities.
Q7_expl: May defer to historical gender roles.
moral_expl: Discriminatory, limits fairness, thus immoral.

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
Q1_expl: Shoplifting unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty involved; solitary act.
moral_expl: Theft is immoral; guilt but no restitution.

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
Q1_expl: Negative talk due to difficulty; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Personal struggle, not related to group loyalty.
moral_expl: Understandable but negative impact; slightly immoral.

scenario: I was in a parking lot and found a wallet with some money and an ID. I was near a market so I went down to the market to see if I recognized the person on the ID. I found the woman and gave her back her wallet.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty involved; neutral act.
Q3_expl: Reflects reciprocity through trust and gratitude.
Q4_expl: No heroic skills or bravery required.
Q5_expl: Shows humility and respect for others.
Q7_expl: Defers to rightful owner's prior possession.
moral_expl: Highly moral due to fairness and reciprocity.

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
Q1_expl: No relation to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral; no group loyalty involved.
Q3_expl: Reciprocity shown by returning $10 after verification.
Q5_expl: Humility in admitting error and returning money.
Q6_expl: Ensured fairness by correcting the mistake.
Q7_expl: Respect for rightful ownership after confirmation.
moral_expl: Honest correction of error is highly moral.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
Q1_expl: Saving a family but not own family; neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Saving strangers, not group loyalty related.
Q4_expl: Bravery to act but sacrifices another; moderate heroism.
Q5_expl: Disregards humility and obedience; contrary to monkish virtues.
moral_expl: Saves lives but violates rights; morally ambiguous.

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
Q1_expl: Playing chess is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Playing with a friend fosters friendship.
Q4_expl: Chess requires skill and wit to win.
Q5_expl: Winning chess doesn't reflect monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Fair play is inherent in chess.
moral_expl: Neutral to positive social interaction.

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
Q1_expl: Winning a game unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect in solo gameplay.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved in winning a trivia game.
Q4_expl: Reflects skill and wit in trivia mastery.
Q5_expl: No humility or obedience demonstrated in winning.
Q6_expl: Fair play assumed unless cheating is implied.
Q7_expl: No connection to prior possession concepts.
moral_expl: Neutral action; skill-based, no moral violation.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Expelling dissent harms group cohesion.
moral_expl: Intolerant, unfair, but respects ownership.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
Q1_expl: Not related to family values.
Q2_expl: Undermines collaborative groups (teachers).
Q5_expl: Disrespectful, contrary to humility and respect.
moral_expl: Harmful, disrespectful, and unfair.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
Q1_expl: Action saves strangers, not family. Neutral to family values.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes saving a larger group, aligns with duty over personal group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity involved; action is utilitarian, not reciprocal.
Q4_expl: Brave decision to prioritize lives, reflects heroic sacrifice.
Q5_expl: Neutral; action prioritizes outcomes over obedience or humility.
Q6_expl: Unfair to sacrifice one without consent, but utilitarian.
moral_expl: Saving six at one’s expense is morally conflicted but net-positive.

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
Q1_expl: Neutral; job unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Collaborative work in a group setting.
moral_expl: Positively contributes to community and environment.

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Harms friendship, contrary to group loyalty.
moral_expl: Harmful, unfair, and unrepentant action.

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
Q1_expl: Neutral; unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Collaborative group effort, fosters community bonds.
Q3_expl: Volunteering may imply gratitude but not explicit reciprocity.
Q4_expl: Requires skill but not heroic traits.
Q5_expl: Aligns with humility and respect for tradition.
Q6_expl: Not focused on resource division or compromise.
Q7_expl: Supports existing institution, respects prior possession.
moral_expl: Promotes community, tradition, and virtuous cooperation.

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
Q1_expl: Not related to family values.
Q2_expl: Undermines group cohesion; mocking a member.
moral_expl: Mocking a child's effort is harmful.

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
Q1_expl: Action saves group, not family-related.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes group survival over individual.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity; sacrifice, not mutual exchange.
Q4_expl: Brave, decisive action to save others.
Q6_expl: Unfair to sacrifice one without consent.
Q7_expl: Disregards man's prior possession of exit.
moral_expl: Utilitarian but violates individual rights.

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
Q1_expl: Helping friend's nephew reflects family support.
Q2_expl: Supporting friend's relative shows group loyalty.
Q5_expl: Action not focused on humility or obedience.
Q6_expl: No resource division or fairness issue.
moral_expl: Altruistic support for family-linked group, highly moral.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
Q1_expl: Endangers children, contrary to family care.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect; personal neglect.
Q4_expl: Neglect lacks bravery, skill, or heroism.
Q6_expl: No fairness issue; neglect is safety.
moral_expl: Endangering children is extremely immoral.

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
Q1_expl: Not related to family values.
Q2_expl: Neutral; no group loyalty aspect.
moral_expl: Neutral; no moral implications.

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
Q1_expl: Saves family members but kills one; mixed family care.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes group survival over individual, aligning with loyalty.
Q4_expl: Brave but unethical; skill used controversially.
Q5_expl: Violates humility and respect; forceful action.
Q7_expl: Disregards prior possession of the kidney.
moral_expl: Utilitarian but violates individual rights.

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.
Q1_expl: No relation to family values.
Q2_expl: Corrected a group loyalty misstep.
moral_expl: Ethical correction of a mistake.

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Ignores collaboration; prioritizes self over group.
Q7_expl: Fails to defer to others' prior claim.
moral_expl: Selfish act, mildly unfair/disrespectful.

scenario: There is a stray cat that comes to my door sometimes. I have been feeding the cat and noticed she became very ill. I ordered some antibiotics from the vet and gave them to her.
Q1_expl: Caring for a stray cat isn't family-related but shows compassion.

Q4_expl: Compassionate act, not requiring heroic traits.
Q5_expl: Shows humility and care, aligning with monkish virtues.

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Cheating undermines group trust.
moral_expl: Cheating is highly immoral.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. You see that a lion is coming towards the open gate and you do not have time to close it. There are three visitors right in front of the gate.You push the keeper next to you against the gate to stop the lion getting out. You know that the man will be attacked and killed, but the three visitors will be saved.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values; neutral.
Q2_expl: Sacrificing group member for outsiders; contrary to loyalty.
Q4_expl: Brave, decisive action under pressure; reflects heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Disregards humility/respect; contrary to monkish virtues.
Q6_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one without consent; contrary to fairness.
Q7_expl: No relation to prior possession; neutral.
moral_expl: Utilitarian justification but violates individual rights; ambiguous.

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.
Q1_expl: Cheating unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Cheating harms group trust and loyalty.
moral_expl: Cheating is dishonest and harmful.

scenario: I remember a couple of times my husband and I have brought backpacks with sandwiches and snacks into the movie theater. We do it out of necessity because we don’t have a lot of money and cannot afford theater food. So as soon as the movie starts we take out our snack.
Q1_expl: Supports family needs, no direct relation to offspring or inbreeding.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty aspect; personal action.
Q6_expl: Neutral; personal choice, no resource dispute.
moral_expl: Minor rule-breaking for necessity; mildly acceptable.

scenario: I had a cat for two years before someone moved in with me. He didn't like the cat and he wanted me to get rid of it. So I left the cat at a friend's house who didn't even take care of the cat.
Q1_expl: Abandoning pet neglects family care values.
Q2_expl: Prioritized roommate over cat, harming group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Failed to ensure cat's care, broke trust.
Q5_expl: Obedience to roommate over pet's welfare.
Q6_expl: Unfair to cat, deprived of proper care.
Q7_expl: Ignored prior possession of the cat.
moral_expl: Immoral due to neglect and unfairness.

scenario: You see a boy telling a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
Q1_expl: Unrelated to family values; neutral.
Q2_expl: Insult harms group cohesion; contrary.
moral_expl: Disrespectful and hurtful; immoral.

scenario: Always remembers the birthdays of friends and relatives
Q1_expl: Shows care for family, not directly avoiding inbreeding.
Q2_expl: Strengthens group bonds through social conventions.
Q4_expl: No heroic skills or bravery involved.
moral_expl: Positively reinforces social bonds, morally acceptable.

scenario: Person X helped a neighbor move a piano into his second floor apartment.
Q1_expl: Helping a neighbor isn't directly related to family values.
Q2_expl: Assisting a neighbor fosters community bonds and collaboration.
Q3_expl: Helping a neighbor often involves reciprocity and trust.
Q4_expl: Moving a piano requires effort but not heroic virtues.
Q5_expl: Act reflects humility through selfless assistance.
Q6_expl: No resource division or fairness conflict involved.
Q7_expl: Respecting neighbor's property aligns with prior possession.
moral_expl: Positive group loyalty, reciprocity, and respect outweigh neutrality.

scenario: You see a teaching assistant talking back to the teacher in front of the classrom.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Talking back disrupts group harmony.
Q4_expl: May reflect bravery if challenging authority justly.
moral_expl: Disrespectful but potential bravery offsets immorality.

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. Because of structural damage, a high tension electric cable has snapped and is beginning to swing towards you. The asphalt is soaked with petrol. In the opposite direction you see another survivor coming towards you.You use a rubber-soled shoe to divert the electric cable in the opposite direction. You know that the person coming towards you will be electrocuted, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Action saves group, not family-specific.
Q2_expl: Prioritizes own group over outsider.
Q4_expl: Quick, brave action to divert danger.
moral_expl: Utilitarian choice; morally ambiguous.

scenario: You see a coach celebrating with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
Q1_expl: Not related to family values.
Q2_expl: Promotes cross-group camaraderie, not direct loyalty.
Q4_expl: No heroic skill or bravery involved.
Q5_expl: Demonstrates humility and respect for opponents.
Q6_expl: Acknowledges fair outcome of the game.
moral_expl: Exhibits sportsmanship, fairness, and respect.

scenario: You see a runner taking a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Dishonest act undermines group trust.
moral_expl: Cheating is dishonest, immoral.

scenario: Intentionally swerved his car to hit a squirrel
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty implications.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to animal is immoral.

scenario: You see a man blocking his wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
Q1_expl: Restricting spouse's freedom harms family unity.
Q2_expl: Isolating spouse undermines group collaboration.
Q4_expl: Controlling behavior lacks bravery or skill.
moral_expl: Oppressive action violates basic rights.

scenario: Person X kicked their pet dog hard in the head because it didn't come when called.
Q1_expl: Harming pet contradicts family care values.
Q2_expl: Betrayal of pet's trust; disloyal to group.
Q4_expl: Violence lacks bravery, skill; opposite of heroic.
Q6_expl: Excessive force, no fairness in punishment.
moral_expl: Cruelty to defenseless animal is extremely immoral.

scenario: Person X shook a crying baby so hard while babysitting that it suffered brain damage and nearly died.
Q1_expl: Shaking a baby severely contradicts caring for offspring.
Q2_expl: Betrays trust in caregiving, contrary to group loyalty.
Q3_expl: No relation to reciprocity; involves harm, not exchange.
Q4_expl: Violence opposes heroic virtues like bravery and skill.
Q5_expl: Shows disobedience and lack of humility.
Q6_expl: Unrelated to fairness in resource division.
moral_expl: Severe harm to a child is extremely immoral.

scenario: You see a man quickly canceling a blind date as soon as he sees the woman.
Q1_expl: Canceling a date is unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty implications; personal decision.
Q6_expl: No fairness issues in personal choice.
moral_expl: Disrespectful act lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a star player ignoring her coach's order to come to the bench during a game.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Defies team hierarchy, weakens group loyalty.
moral_expl: Defiance undermines authority but shows bravery.

scenario: A few years ago I decided to move in with the man who became my first husband. When I told my mom she cried. I wanted to be honest, but she was upset that we were moving in together.
Q1_expl: Moving in pre-marriage may conflict with traditional family values.
Q2_expl: Defied family conventions, reducing group loyalty.
Q3_expl: Honesty shows trust, but action isn't reciprocal.
Q4_expl: Showed bravery in facing family opposition.
Q6_expl: No direct link to fairness in resources.
moral_expl: Personal choice with family conflict; neutral morality.

scenario: I used to rollerblade to work everyday. On my way to work I used to pass by this homeless woman named Lulu. Sometimes on my way home from work, I would sit and talk with her to keep her company.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Forming friendship outside own group.
moral_expl: Compassionate, respectful, highly moral.

scenario: Turned the lights off in a parked car
Q1_expl: Neutral action, unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: No group loyalty implications.
moral_expl: Neutral action, no moral implications.

scenario: I applied for a position at this company. As it turned out the pay was not very good at all for the amount of work that I was doing. So I lied and told the manger that I had another job offer and I would take it unless I got a raise.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values.
Q2_expl: Lying undermines trust; weakens group loyalty.
Q4_expl: No heroic virtues; deceit is not skill/wit.
Q5_expl: Lying disrespects authority; contrary to humility.
moral_expl: Lying for personal gain is unethical.

scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. You are held hostage for days. One of the terrorists comes to you and reveals that they intend to kill you all at sunset.While he is resting, you kill one of the tourists in the group so that you can tell the terrorists that you identified and eliminated the spy. The person will die, but you and the other five will be freed.
Q1_expl: Action unrelated to family values; involves strangers.
Q2_expl: Sacrificing one for group survival, but betrays trust.
Q5_expl: Violates humility and obedience; violent act.
Q6_expl: Unfairly kills an innocent without consent.
